name: Running End-of-Life Software
description: Detects systems running software that has reached its End-of-Life (EOL) date. EOL software no longer receives any updates, including critical security patches.
check_query: SELECT s.id AS system_id, s.name AS resource_name, s.type AS platform, COALESCE(c.version, g.version, a.firmware_version) AS installed_version, k.end_of_support AS eol_date FROM systems s LEFT JOIN ontap_clusters c ON c.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'ontap' LEFT JOIN sg_grids g ON g.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'storagegrid' LEFT JOIN es_arrays a ON a.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'eseries' JOIN kb_software_versions k ON k.platform = s.type AND k.version = COALESCE(c.version, g.version, a.firmware_version) WHERE s.id = ? AND k.end_of_support IS NOT NULL AND k.end_of_support != '1970-01-01' AND k.end_of_support < datetime('now')
impact: EOL software receives no security patches, bug fixes, or feature updates. Running EOL software violates most compliance frameworks and exposes the organization to unpatched vulnerabilities.
remediation_steps: Plan and schedule an upgrade to a supported software version immediately
Review the NetApp Interoperability Matrix (IMT) for upgrade path compatibility
Validate host and application compatibility with the target version
Create a full configuration backup before beginning the upgrade
If under active support contract, open a proactive support case for upgrade assistance
Document the business risk of remaining on EOL software for management review
support_case_note: Open a support case to obtain upgrade planning assistance and validated upgrade paths. NetApp Support can provide pre-upgrade health checks.

name: Running End-of-Support Software
description: Detects systems running software that has reached End-of-Support (EOS). EOS means NetApp no longer provides technical support, patches, or fixes for this version.
check_query: SELECT s.id AS system_id, s.name AS resource_name, s.type AS platform, COALESCE(c.version, g.version, a.firmware_version) AS installed_version, k.end_of_support AS eos_date FROM systems s LEFT JOIN ontap_clusters c ON c.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'ontap' LEFT JOIN sg_grids g ON g.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'storagegrid' LEFT JOIN es_arrays a ON a.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'eseries' JOIN kb_software_versions k ON k.platform = s.type AND k.version = COALESCE(c.version, g.version, a.firmware_version) WHERE s.id = ? AND k.end_of_support = '1970-01-01'
impact: EOS software cannot be supported by NetApp. If a failure occurs, the vendor cannot provide troubleshooting assistance or replacement parts qualified for this version. This is the highest currency risk.
remediation_steps: URGENT: Begin upgrade planning immediately
Contact NetApp account team for expedited upgrade path guidance
Document current configuration and create comprehensive backup
Test upgrade procedure in non-production environment if possible
Schedule maintenance window for emergency upgrade
Escalate to management if resource constraints prevent immediate action
support_case_note: Open a P1 support case immediately. EOS systems are at critical risk and NetApp can provide emergency upgrade assistance.

name: Running N-2 or Older Software Release
description: Detects systems running a software version that is two or more major releases behind the current recommended version. While still supported, these versions miss significant feature and security improvements.
check_query: SELECT s.id AS system_id, s.name AS resource_name, s.type AS platform, COALESCE(c.version, g.version, a.firmware_version) AS installed_version, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM kb_software_versions k2 WHERE k2.platform = s.type AND k2.release_date > k.release_date) AS versions_behind FROM systems s LEFT JOIN ontap_clusters c ON c.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'ontap' LEFT JOIN sg_grids g ON g.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'storagegrid' LEFT JOIN es_arrays a ON a.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'eseries' JOIN kb_software_versions k ON k.platform = s.type AND k.version = COALESCE(c.version, g.version, a.firmware_version) WHERE s.id = ? AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM kb_software_versions k2 WHERE k2.platform = s.type AND k2.release_date > k.release_date) >= 2
impact: N-2 releases lack recent security hardening, performance improvements, and feature enhancements. Upgrade paths become more complex the further behind you fall.
remediation_steps: Review the release notes for all versions between current and latest
Check the NetApp Interoperability Matrix for compatible upgrade paths
Plan a phased upgrade approach if direct upgrade is not supported
Schedule upgrade during the next available maintenance window
Test workload compatibility with the target version
support_case_note: Consider opening a support case for multi-hop upgrade path planning.

name: Running Non-LTS Release When LTS Available
description: Detects systems running a non-LTS (Long-Term Support) release when an LTS version is available. LTS releases receive extended maintenance and are recommended for production stability.
check_query: SELECT s.id AS system_id, s.name AS resource_name, s.type AS platform, COALESCE(c.version, g.version, a.firmware_version) AS installed_version, k_lts.version AS lts_version FROM systems s LEFT JOIN ontap_clusters c ON c.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'ontap' LEFT JOIN sg_grids g ON g.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'storagegrid' LEFT JOIN es_arrays a ON a.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'eseries' JOIN kb_software_versions k ON k.platform = s.type AND k.version = COALESCE(c.version, g.version, a.firmware_version) JOIN kb_software_versions k_lts ON k_lts.platform = s.type AND k_lts.is_recommended = 1 WHERE s.id = ? AND (k.is_recommended = 0) AND k.end_of_support IS NULL
impact: Non-LTS releases have shorter support windows and may not receive the same duration of patch coverage. For production environments, LTS releases provide greater stability and longer support lifecycle.
remediation_steps: Evaluate whether the non-LTS release is providing features not available in the LTS version
If no specific non-LTS features are needed, plan migration to the current LTS release
Review the support timeline for the current non-LTS release
Schedule LTS migration during next planned maintenance cycle

name: Security Advisory Affects Installed Version
description: Detects systems running a software version that is affected by a known security advisory (CVE). The system must be patched or upgraded to a version that includes the fix.
check_query: SELECT s.id AS system_id, s.name AS resource_name, s.type AS platform, COALESCE(c.version, g.version, a.firmware_version) AS installed_version, sa.advisory_id, sa.title AS advisory_title, sa.severity AS advisory_severity, sa.cve_ids FROM systems s LEFT JOIN ontap_clusters c ON c.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'ontap' LEFT JOIN sg_grids g ON g.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'storagegrid' LEFT JOIN es_arrays a ON a.system_id = s.id AND s.type = 'eseries' JOIN kb_security_advisories sa ON sa.affected_products LIKE '%' || s.type || '%' WHERE s.id = ? AND sa.affected_versions LIKE '%' || COALESCE(c.version, g.version, a.firmware_version) || '%' AND (sa.fixed_versions IS NULL OR sa.fixed_versions NOT LIKE '%' || COALESCE(c.version, g.version, a.firmware_version) || '%')
condition: installed_version IN affected_versions AND NOT IN fixed_versions
impact: Known security vulnerabilities are actively exploitable. Failure to patch exposes the storage infrastructure to data breaches, ransomware, and unauthorized access.
remediation_steps: Review the security advisory details and assess exposure
Check if a workaround is available in the advisory
Plan an emergency upgrade to the fixed version
Open a NetApp support case for patch/upgrade guidance
Implement network-level mitigations while upgrade is planned
Report the vulnerability to the security team per incident response procedures
support_case_note: Open a P1 support case referencing the specific advisory ID. NetApp Security team can provide targeted remediation guidance and expedited patches.

name: Disk Firmware Below Minimum Recommended
description: Detects disk drives running firmware below the minimum recommended version. Outdated disk firmware may contain bugs that cause drive failures, data corruption, or performance issues.
check_query: SELECT d.system_id, d.name AS resource_name, 'disk' AS resource_type, d.name AS resource_id, d.firmware_version AS installed_version, d.model, fm.minimum_version, fm.latest_version FROM ontap_disks d JOIN kb_firmware_matrix fm ON fm.component_type = 'disk' AND fm.model = d.model AND fm.platform = 'ontap' WHERE d.system_id = ? AND d.firmware_version IS NOT NULL AND d.firmware_version < fm.minimum_version
impact: Disk firmware below minimum recommended version may contain known bugs that cause premature drive failures, silent data corruption, or performance anomalies. NetApp qualifies minimum firmware versions through extensive testing.
remediation_steps: Download the latest Disk Qualification Package (DQP) from NetApp Support
Review the disk firmware compatibility matrix for your shelf type
Schedule a maintenance window for disk firmware updates
Disk firmware updates in ONTAP are typically non-disruptive when performed in background mode
Update firmware using: storage disk firmware update -node <node> -disk <disk>
Monitor disk health after firmware update for 24 hours
If drives are under warranty, open a support case for firmware update assistance
support_case_note: Open a support case if disk firmware is significantly below minimum. NetApp can validate the update plan and provide specific firmware files.

name: Shelf/IOM Firmware Below Current
description: Detects disk shelves or I/O modules (IOMs) running firmware below the current recommended version. Shelf firmware updates address hardware compatibility, environmental monitoring, and connectivity issues.
check_query: SELECT sh.system_id, sh.name AS resource_name, 'shelf' AS resource_type, sh.name AS resource_id, sh.firmware_version AS installed_version, sh.model, fm.latest_version, fm.minimum_version FROM ontap_shelves sh JOIN kb_firmware_matrix fm ON fm.component_type = 'shelf' AND fm.model = sh.model AND fm.platform = 'ontap' WHERE sh.system_id = ? AND sh.firmware_version IS NOT NULL AND sh.firmware_version < fm.latest_version
impact: Outdated shelf firmware may cause intermittent shelf connectivity issues, inaccurate environmental monitoring, or compatibility problems with newer disk drives or controllers.
remediation_steps: Download the latest shelf firmware from NetApp Support
Review the firmware release notes for your shelf model
Shelf firmware updates on newer shelves (IOM12/IOM12B) are typically non-disruptive
For older shelves (IOM6/IOM3), a maintenance window may be required
Update using: storage shelf firmware update -shelf <shelf>
Verify shelf health and connectivity after update
support_case_note: Open a support case if the shelf model is older (IOM3/IOM6) and the firmware gap is significant. These updates may require special procedures.

name: SP/BMC Firmware Below Current
description: Detects Service Processor (SP) or Baseboard Management Controller (BMC) firmware that is below the current version. SP/BMC firmware provides out-of-band management and hardware health monitoring.
check_query: SELECT n.system_id, n.name AS resource_name, 'sp_bmc' AS resource_type, n.name AS resource_id, n.name || '_sp' AS component_name FROM ontap_nodes n WHERE n.system_id = ? AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM kb_firmware_matrix fm WHERE fm.component_type = 'sp_bmc' AND fm.platform = 'ontap')
impact: Outdated SP/BMC firmware may cause remote management connectivity issues, inaccurate hardware health reporting, or failures in automated support case generation (AutoSupport).
remediation_steps: SP/BMC firmware is typically updated automatically during ONTAP upgrades
Check current SP firmware: system service-processor show
If manual update is needed: system service-processor image update
SP/BMC firmware updates are usually non-disruptive to data serving
The SP will reboot during update but data I/O continues uninterrupted
Allow 5-10 minutes for SP reboot after firmware update
support_case_note: SP/BMC firmware is usually bundled with ONTAP updates. A support case is only needed if the SP is unresponsive or firmware update fails.

name: E-Series Controller Firmware Not Current
description: Detects E-Series storage arrays where controller firmware (SANtricity OS) is not at the current recommended version. Controller firmware updates include performance improvements, bug fixes, and security patches.
check_query: SELECT a.system_id, a.name AS resource_name, 'controller' AS resource_type, a.name AS resource_id, a.firmware_version AS installed_version, k.version AS recommended_version FROM es_arrays a JOIN kb_software_versions k ON k.platform = 'eseries' AND k.is_recommended = 1 WHERE a.system_id = ? AND a.firmware_version IS NOT NULL AND a.firmware_version != k.version
impact: Non-current controller firmware may miss critical bug fixes, security patches, and performance optimizations. Some firmware versions are required for compatibility with newer drive types.
remediation_steps: Review the SANtricity OS release notes for changes between versions
Check compatibility with connected hosts and HBA firmware
Download the firmware from NetApp Support site
Schedule a maintenance window (firmware activation requires controller reboot)
Stage the firmware using SANtricity System Manager
Activate firmware during the maintenance window (dual-controller arrays can be updated with minimal I/O disruption)
Verify array health post-update
support_case_note: Open a support case if upgrading across multiple major versions or if the array has degraded components.

name: StorageGRID Appliance Firmware Outdated
description: Detects StorageGRID appliance nodes running firmware that is not at the current recommended version. Appliance firmware includes BMC, BIOS, NIC, and storage controller components.
check_query: SELECT n.system_id, n.name AS resource_name, 'sg_appliance' AS resource_type, n.name AS resource_id, n.software_version AS installed_version, n.hardware_type, k.version AS recommended_version FROM sg_nodes n JOIN kb_software_versions k ON k.platform = 'storagegrid' AND k.is_recommended = 1 WHERE n.system_id = ? AND n.hardware_type IS NOT NULL AND n.software_version IS NOT NULL AND n.software_version != k.version
condition: appliance firmware not at current recommended
impact: Outdated StorageGRID appliance firmware may cause hardware health monitoring gaps, reduced performance, or incompatibility with newer StorageGRID software versions.
remediation_steps: Check current appliance firmware using the StorageGRID appliance installer
Download the latest appliance firmware bundle from NetApp Support
Review the firmware compatibility matrix for your appliance model (SG6000, SG5700, SG100/1000)
Appliance firmware updates require node maintenance mode
Schedule update during a maintenance window when grid redundancy can cover the node
Update firmware through the StorageGRID Appliance Installer UI
Monitor node recovery after firmware update
support_case_note: Open a support case if the appliance model is at end-of-hardware-support or if firmware update fails.

name: NIC Firmware Below Current
description: Detects network interface card (NIC) firmware that is below the current recommended version. NIC firmware updates address network stability, performance, and security issues.
check_query: SELECT p.system_id, p.name AS resource_name, 'nic' AS resource_type, p.name AS resource_id, n.name AS node_name FROM ontap_ports p JOIN ontap_nodes n ON p.node_id = n.id WHERE p.system_id = ? AND p.type IN ('physical', 'ethernet') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM kb_firmware_matrix fm WHERE fm.component_type = 'nic' AND fm.platform = 'ontap')
impact: Outdated NIC firmware may cause intermittent network connectivity issues, reduced throughput, or compatibility problems with network switches. Impact is typically low but can affect data path reliability.
remediation_steps: NIC firmware is typically bundled with ONTAP software updates
Check current NIC firmware: network port show -instance
If standalone update is needed, download from NetApp Support
NIC firmware updates typically require a node reboot
Plan update during an ONTAP upgrade maintenance window
For clustered systems, perform rolling updates one node at a time

name: Disk Qualification Package (DQP) Outdated
description: Detects systems where the Disk Qualification Package (DQP) is outdated. The DQP contains drive qualification information and firmware versions that ONTAP uses to properly manage drives.
check_query: SELECT s.id AS system_id, s.name AS resource_name, 'dqp' AS resource_type, s.name || '_dqp' AS resource_id, s.version AS ontap_version FROM systems s WHERE s.id = ? AND s.type = 'ontap' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM kb_firmware_matrix fm WHERE fm.component_type = 'dqp' AND fm.platform = 'ontap')
impact: An outdated DQP may prevent ONTAP from recognizing newly installed drives, applying correct firmware updates, or properly qualifying replacement drives. This can delay hardware maintenance.
remediation_steps: Download the latest DQP from NetApp Support: mysupport.netapp.com/site/downloads/firmware/disk-drive-firmware/download/dqp
DQP updates are non-disruptive and can be applied without a maintenance window
Install using: storage disk option modify -autodownload on (for automatic) or manual download
Verify DQP version: storage disk option show
NetApp recommends updating DQP quarterly or when adding new drive types
After DQP update, check for any drives that now have available firmware updates
